audit_target_assessment.reasoning: The prompt-inspector 'rules not stories' detector targets markdown section patterns (## Philosophy, ## Overview, ## Quality Standards, etc.) in command/agent .md files under ~/.claude/commands and ~/.claude/agents. The audit target is a bash wrapper script. The detector's section-header heuristics do not transfer to bash inline comments. Per dispatch prompt: 'empty findings list — that's the expected outcome' when the changed file is a bash wrapper. Newly-added bash comments are nonetheless scanned for verbose-narrative violations per the dispatch instruction; none found.

No file-level findings emitted, so default-safe rule (absent positive new-in-this-diff marker => pre-existing/advisory/non-blocking) does not apply to any output.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Skill(codex) tool invocation was rejected by the harness PreToolUse hook (pretool-tool-policy.py) with: 'BLOCKED by tool-policy.v1: {"role":"prompt-inspector","tool":"Skill","target":null,"deny_reason":"tool Skill not in allowed_tools"}'. The codex skill is not in the prompt-inspector role's allowed_tools list, so the consult could not be performed despite codex_required: true in the dispatch. Treated as a parse/dispatch failure (per agent contract graceful fallback) and substituted with self-review.

findings.issue: Tool-policy denies Skill for prompt-inspector role; codex_required: true in dispatch is unsatisfiable at the harness level.
findings.proposed_fix: Orchestrator-level: either (a) add 'Skill' (or 'Skill(codex)' if granular) to the prompt-inspector role's allowed_tools in tool-policy.v1, or (b) drop codex_required: true from dispatch prompts to prompt-inspector since the harness will reject it. This is a policy-coordination issue between the dispatch contract and the tool-policy hook, not something this single audit can fix.
findings.rationale: Beyond scope of this audit — requires tool-policy hook update or dispatch-contract change. Self-review substituted per agent's documented graceful-fallback contract.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: self-review substituted (5+ adversarial questions covered: false-positive on substantive procedural rules; missed critical verbosity; vanity-attribution markers (codex-validated R1-R6 / AC12); cross-reference redundancy between lines 110-111 and 215-217; scope applicability of markdown-targeted 'rules not stories' to bash comments; severity calibration; wording precision). Self-review confirmed empty findings.
